div.h2: http_conn_t Memory Layout — Cache Line Boundaries

div.p: Colors: 🟣 Header/Fields (#8e44ad) 🔵 Data (#2980b9) 🟠 Pointers (#e67e22) ⬜ Padding (#7f8c8d) 🟢 Success/Structs (#27ae60)

g.text: Cache Line 0 [0x00 - 0x3F] ← HOT: Fits in L1

ol.li: L1 Alignment: Hot fields packed in CL 0 prevents "false sharing" and minimizes L1 misses on the main loop.
Demand Paging: read_buf and header_buf are 24KB. On Linux, these pages are only allocated physically when touched. Idle connections effectively consume only ~2KB physical RAM.

text.tspan: Sequential access patterns used during epoll_wait cycle.
Demand-paged: Zero overhead for idle connections.
Flat storage for parsed header strings.
Pointers in http_request_t reference this region.
